question_text: A gamma ray has a frequency of 3 × 10²⁰ Hz and a radio wave has a frequency of 1 × 10⁶ Hz. Calculate the wavelength of each wave (speed of EM waves = 3 × 10⁸ m/s). Which is longer?
correct_answer: Gamma ray wavelength = 1 × 10⁻¹² m. Radio wave wavelength = 3 × 10² m (or 300 m). The radio wave has a much longer wavelength.
explanation: Using wavelength = speed ÷ frequency: Gamma ray = (3 × 10⁸) ÷ (3 × 10²⁰) = 1 × 10⁻¹² m. Radio wave = (3 × 10⁸) ÷ (1 × 10⁶) = 3 × 10² m. The radio wave is about a trillion trillion times longer!
